---
title: "2.8 for Loops"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### `for` loops package counting logic into the header

A <span class="term">for loop</span> (for 循环) is usually used when the number of repetitions is known or countable.

![Loop in App Inventor](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-8-for-loops/loopAppInv.png){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

```java
for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++)
{
    System.out.println(i);
}
```

## Three Parts

### Initialization, condition, update

![Compare for and while](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-8-for-loops/compareForAndWhile.png){fig-align="center" width="46%"}

```java
for (int i = 1; i <= 5; i++)
{
    System.out.println(i);
}
```

::: {.tight-list}
- `int i = 1`: initialize once
- `i <= 5`: test before each repetition
- `i++`: update after each body execution
:::

## For Loop Flow

### Same loop idea, compact syntax

![For loop flow](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-8-for-loops/ForLoopFlow.png){fig-align="center" width="42%"}

The condition still decides whether the loop body runs again. The update still must move the loop toward stopping.

## Code Task

### Convert `while` to `for`

`while` version:

```java
int count = 1;
while (count <= 5)
{
    System.out.println(count);
    count++;
}
```

Equivalent `for` version:

```java
for (int count = 1; count <= 5; count++)
{
    System.out.println(count);
}
```

## Quick Check

### Count iterations

How many times does the loop body run?

```java
for (int i = 0; i < 10; i += 2)
{
    System.out.println(i);
}
```

Answer: <span class="mark">5</span> times. The values are `0`, `2`, `4`, `6`, `8`.

## Mixed-Up Code

### Print even numbers

Reorder the lines to print even numbers from `2` through `10`.

```java
public class PrintEvens
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        for (int num = 2; num <= 10; num += 2)
        {
            System.out.println(num);
        }
    }
}
```

Key: the update is `num += 2`.

## Decrementing Loops

### A loop can count down

```java
for (int bottles = 5; bottles > 0; bottles--)
{
    System.out.println(bottles + " bottles");
}
```

Trace the header:

::: {.tight-list}
- starts at `5`
- continues while greater than `0`
- subtracts one after each pass
:::

## Quick Check

### Equivalent loop reasoning

Which loop prints the same values as `count = 2, 4, 6`?

```java
for (int count = 2; count <= 6; count += 2)
{
    System.out.println(count);
}
```

Equivalent:

```java
int count = 2;
while (count <= 6)
{
    System.out.println(count);
    count += 2;
}
```

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle drawing shapes

Use a `for` loop to draw a polygon.

```java
int sides = 6;
for (int i = 1; i <= sides; i++)
{
    yertle.forward(50);
    yertle.turn(360 / sides);
}
```

Change `sides` to draw a triangle, square, pentagon, and hexagon.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- identify the initialization, condition, and update in a `for` loop
- convert between equivalent `while` and `for` loops
- count loop iterations from start, stop, and update values
- trace incrementing and decrementing loops
- choose `for` when the repetition count is known or countable
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
